About/index.title: About
About/index.content:
About
I’m Ashwin.
I work at the intersection of product and engineering, and I’m currently focused on going deeper into machine learning and AI systems.
This site is part of that process.
What this site is
This is my working knowledge system, published.
I use it to:

break down complex topics into simple mental models
document what I learn as I learn it
turn messy notes into structured understanding
share projects and experiments

What I’m working on

Building a strong foundation in machine learning and deep learning
Applying ML concepts through hands-on projects
Exploring AI systems and how they are built end-to-end

What you’ll see here

Notes that evolve over time
Cheatsheets designed for recall and interviews
Project logs with real decisions and tradeoffs
Occasional long-form writing

Why this exists
Writing forces clarity.
Publishing creates accountability.
Over time, this becomes a map of how I think and what I’ve built.

If you’re working on similar things, this site should be useful to you.

Blog/index.title: Blog
Blog/index.content:
Blog
Longer-form essays, reflections, and explanations.
This is the slower, more narrative side of the site.

Cheatsheets/index.title: Cheatsheets
Cheatsheets/index.content:
Cheatsheets
Compact notes designed for quick recall.
These are the pieces I want to be able to revisit quickly before interviews, projects, or study sessions.

ML-Notes/index.title: ML Notes
ML-Notes/index.content:
ML Notes
Notes on machine learning, deep learning, and AI systems.
This section is where I try to turn confusion into clarity — one concept at a time.

Projects/index.title: Projects
Projects/index.content:
Projects
Build logs, experiments, and technical writeups.
This is where ideas get tested against reality.

index.title: Ashwin's Digital Garden
index.content:
Ashwin’s Digital Garden
I use this space to think in public.
This is where I document what I’m learning, building, and figuring out — mainly in machine learning, AI systems, product thinking, and technical projects.
Most of what you’ll find here started as rough notes. The useful ones get refined into cheatsheets, deeper explanations, and project writeups.
What you’ll find here

ML Notes — concept notes, intuition-first explanations, and deeper breakdowns
Cheatsheets — compact references for fast review
Projects — builds, experiments, and lessons learned
Blog — longer-form essays and reflections

How to read this site
This is not a polished blog in the traditional sense.
It is a working knowledge system:

ideas evolve
notes get rewritten
links between topics grow over time

If something feels unfinished, that usually means I’m still learning through it.
Start here
